content:
💡 DroidDesk | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: Transforming an Android phone into a portable Linux PC capable of running a wide range of desktop applications.
✨ Key Features: • Run full Linux desktop on Android • Direct kernel access, no emulation • Support for XFCE4, LXQt, MATE, KDE desktop environments
📖 Summary: DroidDesk enables users to run a full Linux desktop environment on their Android phone, leveraging Termux, Termux X11, and Proot. It allows for the installation and execution of various Linux applications, including development tools, office suites, and even demanding software like Blender, with support for both on-device display and external monitor output via USB-C or a Raspberry Pi bridge.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 vibe-draw | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To enable users with little to no 3D modeling experience to easily create and build 3D worlds from simple 2D sketches.
✨ Key Features: • Sketching on a 2D canvas • AI-powered drawing enhancement • 2D sketch to 3D model conversion • 3D world building • Iterative editing of 3D models via sketching or text prompts
📖 Summary: Vibe Draw is a TypeScript-based application that transforms rough 2D sketches into detailed 3D models and worlds. It allows users to draw, enhance, convert to 3D, build within a 3D environment, and export their creations in glTF format.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🚀 mac-audit | Shell
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide users with a detailed inventory and analysis of all software and related components installed on their macOS system, aiding in system management, cleanup, and understanding.
✨ Key Features: • Audits applications (Applications folder, Homebrew Casks, App Store, Login Items, VS Code extensions) • Scans various programming language installations (Python, Node.js, Rust, Go, Ruby, Java, PHP, etc.)
📖 Summary: mac-audit is a comprehensive shell script designed to perform a full audit of installed software on macOS. It identifies applications, programming languages, AI/LLM models, Docker components, DevOps tools, security utilities, and disk usage, providing details on what is installed, where it is located, and its purpose.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 qnote | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: A lightweight, feature-rich desktop application for writing, organizing, and exporting notes in plain text and Markdown formats.
✨ Key Features: • Plain text and Markdown editing • Live Markdown preview • Formatting toolbar • File history with search • Version history
📖 Summary: qnote is a minimal, cross-platform desktop note editor built with Tauri and React. It focuses on providing a clean interface for writing and managing notes, with features like Markdown support, live preview, file history, and PDF/HTML export.
🔗 Links: • View Project ================ 🔓 Open Source
